ksa_text: Knowledge of AI regulatory frameworks and compliance requirements, including the EU AI Act risk classification structure, conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, high-risk system obligations, documentation requirements, technical documentation standards, and obligations on providers versus deployers.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although AI Governance is an edge domain for the VP Analytics, this knowledge KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.524) and conceptual alignment to include at the vp level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.524). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the VP Analytics versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act (Article 9), and OECD AI Principles confirm AI governance as a critical competency for responsible AI leadership.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with AI Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for VP Analytics.

decision_rationale: Edge-domain AI Governance knowledge KSA cleared adversarial review with score 0.524. Included based on sufficient framework alignment and role relevance for the VP Analytics.
